PANAMA CANAL.
1. MULTI-NATION NAVAL CONTROL OF SHIPPING (NCS) AUTHORITIES
   WILL BE CONDUCTING AN EXERCISE FROM 19-24 AUGUST 2011.
   THE EXERCISE WILL BE ONGOING IN THE BROAD VICINITY OF
   APPROACHES TO THE PANAMA CANAL.
2. AN NCAGS REGION WILL BE DECLARED EFFECTIVE AS OF 19 AUGUST
   IN THE INTERNATIONAL AND COASTAL WATERS APPROACHING THE
   PANAMA CANAL, BOTH ATLANTIC AND PACIFIC.  A MULTI-NATIONAL
   COALITION NAVAL FORCE AND NOTIONAL RISK AREAS WILL BE
   PRESENT IN THESE WATERS. THE REGION WILL BE DISESTABLISHED
   AS OF 24 AUGUST 2011.
3. MERCHANT AND COMMERCIAL SHIPS TRANSITING THESE AREAS MAY
   BE REQUESTED TO REPORT VESSEL INFORMATION UTILIZING FORMAT
   ALPHA REPORTING FORMAT AS OUTLINED IN ALLIED TECHNICAL
   PUBLICATION ATP2(B) VOL 2- NAVAL CO-OPERATION AND GUIDANCE
   FOR SHIPPING MANUAL (NCAGS)- GUIDE TO OWNERS, OPERATORS,
   MASTERS AND OFFICERS. BASIC INFORMATION INCLUDES:
   A. SHIP NAME
   B. CALLSIGN/IMO#
   C. E-MAIL ADDRESS OF VESSEL
   D. TYPE OF SHIP (E.G. TANKER, CONTAINER, BULK, FISHING.)
   E. TYPE OF CARGO
   F. POSITION, COURSE AND SPEED AT TIME OF REPORTING
   G. PREVIOUS PORT OF CALL
   H. NEXT PORT OF CALL
   SHIP INFORMATION WILL BE HANDLED AS BUSINESS AND SECURITY
   SENSITIVE INFORMATION. REPORTS DURING THE EXERCISE SHOULD
   BE MADE TO THE SHIPPING COORDINATOR AT THE FOLLOWING
   E-MAIL ADDRESS: SCT@NAVYTELEX.US
4. SAFETY IS PARAMOUNT. THE EXERCISE WILL NOT EMPLOY ACTUAL
   CHANGES TO MARSEC LEVEL OR SHIPBOARD EMERGENCY ALERT
   SIGNALS. NAVAL GUIDANCE TO MERCHANT SHIPPING IS ADVISORY
   ONLY. MERCHANT PARTICIPATION IS STRICTLY VOLUNTARY.
5. MERCHANCT VESSELS INTERESTED IN VOLUNTARY EXERCISE
   PARTICIPATION DURING THE EXERCISE PERIOD SHOULD CONTACT
   JECG@NAVYTELEX.US. THE HIGHEST CONSIDERATION FOR VOYAGE
   SCHEDULES AND EFFICIENCIES IS RECOGNIZED. AS PRACTICAL,
   NCAGS WILL ASSIST DOCUMENTATION OF SHIP PARTICIPATION
   FOR SECURITY DRILL PURPOSES.
6. VESSELS WITHIN THE NCAGS SHIPPING AREA DURING THE EXERCISE
   DATES WITH QUESTIONS OR CONCERNS CAN CONTACT THE PANAMA
   LOCATED NCAGS SHIPPING CONTROL TEAM (SCT) VIA
   E-MAIL TO: SCT@NAVYTELEX.US.
7. FOR FURTHER INFORMATION REGARDING THIS ADVISORY, CONTACT
   CAPTAIN ROBERT FORD, MARITIME ADMINISTRATION, OFFICE OF
   SECURITY, CODE: MAR-420, ROOM W25-308, 1200 NEW JERSEY AVE, S.E.,
   WASHINGTION, DC 20590, PHONE: 202 366 0223, FAX: 202 366 3954,
   TELEX: 7108229426 (MARAD DOT WSH) OR E-MAIL: MARADSECURITY@DOT.GOV.
8. CANCEL THIS MSG 25 AUG.

BALTIC SEA.
1. EXERCISE NORTHERN COASTS 2011/DANEX 2011
   IS A LIVE MULTINATIONAL MARITIME EXERCISE
   TAKING PLACE FROM 12 THRU 23 SEP IN
   THE NORTHERN, SOUTHERN AND WESTERN BALTIC,
   GERMAN BALTIC CONTINENTAL SHELF, SKAGERRAK,
   KATTEGAT AND BALTIC ENTRANCE. A LARGE NUMBER
   OF AIR, SURFACE AND SUBSURFACE UNITS FROM
   AT LEAST 20 NATIONS ARE PARTICIPATING.
   MINE WARFARE OPERATIONS INCLUDING LAYING AND
   RECOVERY OF EXERCISE MINES AND GUNNERY
   EXERCISES WILL ALSO TAKE PLACE. SHIPS
   ENTERING THIS AREA ARE REQUESTED TO NAVIGATE
   WITH CAUTION, TO KEEP CLEAR OF NAVAL SHIPS
   AND TO PAY ATTENTION TO THEIR SIGNALS.
   DURING THE EXERCISE COOPERATION WITH MERCHANT
   VESSELS (NAVAL COOPERATION AND GUIDANCE FOR
   SHIPPING/NCAGS) WILL BE EXERCISED TO A
   LIMITED EXTENT. MERCHANT SHIPS VOLUNTARILY
   PARTICIPATING IN INTERACTION WITH MILITARY
   FORCES ARE KINDLY ASKED TO COOPERATE ON
   INFORMATION EXCHANGE OR LIVE BOARDING
   REQUESTS. PARTICIPATION IN THE EXERCISE IS
   ENTIRELY VOLUNTARY AND AT THE PARTICIPANTS
   OWN COST AND RESPONSIBILITY.
   FOR VOLUNTARY PARTICIPATION BY MERCHANT
   VESSELS, THE FOLLOWING REPORTING PROCEDURES
   WILL APPLY:

EASTERN MEDITERRANEAN SEA.
LIBYA.
1. MARITIME EMBARGO:
   NATO IS COORDINATING A MARITIME EMBARGO OPERATION
   IN VICINITY OF LIBYA IN ACCORDANCE WITH
   UNITED NATIONS SECURITY COUNCIL RESOLUTIONS
   (UNSCR) 1970, 1973 AND 2009.
2. EMBARGO ENFORCEMENT:
   THE OPERATIONS ARE BEING CONDUCTED IN SUPPORT OF
   THE EMBARGO IN THE MARITIME SURVEILLANCE AREA (MSA)
   ENCLOSED BY THE FOLLOW POINTS, CLOCKWISE FROM POINT A:
   A. 34-00N 012-00E, B. 34-00N 022-00E,
   C. 33-00N 025-00E AND THEN DIRECTLY SOUTH TO
      LIMIT OF LIBYAN TERRITORIAL WATERS AND THEN
      FOLLOWING LIMIT OF LIBYAN TERRITORIAL WATERS
      WESTWARDS TO LIMIT OF LIBYAN TERRITORIAL WATERS
      AT 12-00E DIRECTLY SOUTH OF POINT A.
   THE MSA IS IDENTICAL TO THAT DEFINED IN
   THE CANCELED HYDROLANT 587/11.
3. REPORTING SYSTEM:
   ALL VESSELS PLANNING TO TRANSIT THROUGH THE MSA
   ARE REQUIRED TO SEND THE DATA SPECIFIED TO THE
   NATO SHIPPING CENTRE (UK) AND TO NATO HQ
   MC NAPLES, 12 HOURS BEFORE ENTERING THE MSA.
   DATA TO SEND:
   A. SHIP NAME.
   B. FLAG.
   C. IMO NUMBER.
   D. INMARSAT PHONE NUMBER.
   E. SHIP'S CURRENT POSITION AND TIME OF SENDING MESSAGE.
   F. CURRENT COURSE.
   G. CURRENT SPEED.
   H. CARGO.
   I. DESTINATION AND ESTIMATED DATE AND TIME OF ARRIVAL.
   J. LAST PORT CALLED AT, AND THE DATE AND TIME OF
      DEPARTURE FROM THAT PORT.
4. GENERAL GUIDANCE FOR MARINERS:
   NATO NAVAL UNITS WITHIN THE MSA WILL HAIL AND MAY
   BOARD VESSELS. WILLINGNESS TO COOPERATE AND QUICK
   RESPONSE TO HAILING WILL MINIMIZE DELAYS TO MERCHANT
   SHIPPING TRAFFIC. FOR ADDITIONAL INFORMATION,
   A DEDICATED NATO NAVAL COOPERATION AND GUIDANCE FOR
   SHIPPING (NCAGS) TEAM CAN BE CONTACTED DIRECTLY
   0430Z TO 1630Z AT NATO HQ MC NAPLES. THE NCAGS TEAM'S
   ROLE IS TO ENHANCE COORDINATION BETWEEN NATO UNITS
   ENFORCING THE EMBARGO AND MARINERS TRANSITING THE MSA.
   ALSO, THE NATO HQ MC NAPLES MARITIME OPERATION CENTRE
   (MOC) CAN BE CONTACTED 24 HOURS A DAY. NATO HQ MC NAPLES
   RECOMMENDS THAT MERCHANT SHIPPING COMPANIES WITH VESSELS
   PLANNING TO CALL AT LIBYAN HARBORS CONTACT THE NCAGS
   TEAM AT NATO HQ MC NAPLES.
5. CONTACT DETAILS:
   NATO SHIPPING CENTRE (UK),
   PHONE: 44 192 395 6574, E-MAIL: INFO@SHIPPING.NATO.INT,
   WEBPAGE: HTTP:WWW.SHIPPING.NATO.INT/NATO HQ MC NAPLES,
   MOC TOLL FREE PHONE: 800 1101 2010,
   NCAGS: 39 081 721 6184, E-MAIL: MOCN3DOGCELL@MANP.NATO.INT.
6. CANCEL HYDROLANT 587/11.

HORN OF AFRICA.
1. THIS MARAD ADVISORY PROVIDES INFORMATION ON THE RISK
   TO VESSELS TRANSITING THE HIGH RISK WATERS OF THE
   GULF OF ADEN (GOA), RED SEA, THE INDIAN OCEAN,
   ARABIAN SEA AND WATERS OFF THE HORN OF AFRICA (SOMALIA).
2. THIS ADVISORY WILL BE PUBLISHED ON THE MARAD WEB SITE
   AT WWW.MARAD.DOT.GOV UNDER THE HORN OF AFRICA PIRACY
   PORTAL AND ON THE U.S. COAST GUARD HOMEPORT SITE AT
   HOMEPORT.USCG.MIL/PIRACY. OTHER PERTINENT INFORMATION
   IS ALSO POSTED ON THESE WEB SITES.
3. U.S. FLAG OPERATORS WITH SHIPS IN THE AFFECTED AREAS
   ARE REQUESTED TO FORWARD THIS ADVISORY TO THEIR SHIPS
   BY THE MOST EXPEDITIOUS MEANS.
4. U.S. FLAG SHIPS OPERATING IN HIGH RISK WATERS ARE
   REQUIRED TO COMPLY WITH U.S. COAST GUARD MARITIME
   SECURITY (MARSEC) DIRECTIVE 104-6 (CURRENT VERSION).
   GUIDANCE, ADVISORIES AND LINKS TO ASSIST U.S. FLAG
   VESSELS IN COMPLYING WITH THE MARSEC DIRECTIVE AND
   IN COMBATING PIRACY MAY BE ACCESSED ON HOMEPORT AT
   HTTP://HOMEPORT.USCG.MIL/PIRACY. QUESTIONS MAY BE
   DIRECTED TO LCDR JAMES FOGLE, U.S. COAST GUARD,
   PHONE: 202 372 1038, E-MAIL: JAMES.T.FOGLE@USCG.MIL.
   NONE OF THE GUIDANCE IN THIS NOTICE IS MEANT TO
   SUPERCEDE MARSEC DIRECTIVE 104-6 (CURRENT VERSION)
   OR SUPPORTING PORT SECURITY ADVISORIES.
5. PIRATES ARE ATTACKING VESSELS, INCLUDING YACHTS AND
   RECREATIONAL CRAFT, IN THE GULF OF ADEN, OFF THE SOMALI
   COAST AND IN THE INDIAN OCEAN. ATTACKS HAVE OCCURRED
   OVER 1000 NAUTICAL MILES AWAY FROM SOMALIA, OFF THE
   COAST OF OMAN AND IN THE ARABIAN SEA LESS THAN 200 MILES
   FROM THE COAST OF INDIA. ATTACKS HAVE ALSO TAKEN PLACE
   OFF THE KENYAN AND TANZANIAN COASTS, SOUTHERN RED SEA,
   SEYCHELLES, MALDIVES, MADAGASCAR AND AN ATTACK IN THE
   MOZAMBIQUE CHANNEL. THESE PIRATES ARE FIRING AUTOMATIC
   WEAPONS AND ROCKET PROPELLED GRENADES (RPG) IN AN
   ATTEMPT TO BOARD AND HIJACK VESSELS. ONCE THE ATTACK IS
   SUCCESSFUL AND THE VESSEL HIJACKED, THE PIRATES DIRECT
   THE VESSEL TO THE SOMALI COAST AND THEREAFTER DEMAND
   RANSOM FOR THE SAFE RELEASE OF THE VESSEL AND CREW.
   PIRATES HAVE INCREASINGLY USED HIJACKED FISHING VESSELS
   AND MERCHANT VESSELS TO CONDUCT PIRACY OPERATIONS AS
   MOTHER VESSELS TO SAIL FAR FROM THE SOMALI COAST TO
   ATTACK AND HIJACK PASSING VESSELS. SMALLER SKIFFS ARE
   LAUNCHED FROM THE PIRATE MOTHER VESSEL TO ATTACK THE
   MERCHANT VESSELS. PIRATES ARE HEAVILY ARMED WITH
   AUTOMATIC WEAPONS AND RPGS.
6. DESPITE THE INCREASE IN PRESENCE AND EFFECTIVENESS OF
   NAVAL FORCES IN THE REGION, AS WELL AS THE EFFECTIVENESS
   OF DEFENSIVE AND PROTECTIVE MEASURES, PIRATE ACTIVITY
   HAS CONTINUED AND COMMERCIAL AND CIVILIAN SHIPS CONTINUE
   TO BE ATTACKED AND SEIZED.
7. TRANSIT BY YACHTS AND PRIVATELY OWNED SAILING VESSELS
   THROUGH HIGH RISK WATERS IS EXTREMELY HAZARDOUS AND MAY
   RESULT IN CAPTURE BY PIRATES. VESSELS THAT MAKE THIS
   PASSAGE DESPITE THIS WARNING SHOULD MAKE CONTACT IN
   ADVANCE WITH THE NAVAL AUTHORITIES. IN ADDITION, AMERICAN
   CITIZENS ABOARD SHOULD INFORM THE NEAREST U.S. EMBASSY OR
   CONSULATE OF THEIR PLANS TO TRANSIT THE AREA AND/OR
   UPDATE THEIR INFORMATION VIA THE SMART TRAVELER
   ENROLLMENT PROGRAM (STEP) ON WWW.TRAVEL.STATE.GOV.
   BASIC GUIDANCE FOR TRANSIT CAN BE FOUND AT
   HTTP://WWW.MSCHOA.ORG/YACHTINGGUIDANCE/PAGES/
   YACHTINGGUIDANCEONE.ASPX.
   IF YOU ARE DUE TO TRAVEL THE AREA OF HIGH THREAT, PLEASE
   INFORM MSC(HOA) BY E-MAILING POSTMASTER@MSCHOA.ORG
   WITH THE SUBJECT LINE "YACHT VESSEL MOVEMENT".
8. RELATED COAST GUARD NOTICE: THE U.S. COAST GUARD
   RECENTLY ISSUED A NOTICE TO MARINERS ADVISORY FOR YACHTS
   AND SAILING VESSELS. THE COAST GUARD ADVISES THAT YACHTS
   AND SAILING VESSELS ARE PARTICULARLY VULNERABLE TO ATTACK
   DUE TO THEIR LOW SPEED AND LOW FREEBOARD. THE COAST GUARD
   ADVISES AGAINST ALL OPERATION OF OR TRAVEL BY YACHT AND
   PLEASURE CRAFT ON THE HIGH SEAS IN THE GULF OF ADEN,
   ARABIAN SEA, SOMALI BASIN AND THE WESTERN PARTS OF THE
   INDIAN OCEAN. ALL YACHTS AND SAILING VESSELS INTENDING
   TO SAIL THROUGH HIGH RISK AREAS SHOULD RECONSIDER THE
   NECESSITY OF THEIR TRAVEL AND ALTERNATIVES, SUCH AS
   TRANSPORTING THE VESSEL BY YACHT CARRIER. THE COAST GUARD
   FURTHER ADVISES THAT PARTICIPANTS IN YACHT RACES AND
   RALLIES IN THESE HIGH RISK AREAS ARE AT GREAT RISK OF
   ATTACK. THESE EVENTS ARE OFTEN PUBLICIZED AND COULD DRAW
   ATTENTION FROM PIRATES, DESPITE THE SECURITY AND SAFETY
   MEASURES THAT MIGHT BE PUT IN PLACE. THE COAST GUARD
   STRONGLY URGES MARINERS NOT TO PARTICIPATE IN THESE
   EVENTS THAT WILL REQUIRE TRANSIT THROUGH HIGH RISK
   WATERS. FOR THE TEXT OF THE COAST GUARD NOTICE, PLEASE
   GO TO HTTP://HOMEPORT.USCG.MIL/PIRACY.
9. HOWEVER, AMERICAN CITIZENS ABOARD YACHTS AND SAILING
   VESSELS SHOULD INFORM THE NEAREST U.S. EMBASSY OR
   CONSULATE OF THEIR PLANS TO TRANSIT THE AREA AND/OR
   UPDATE THEIR INFORMATION VIA THE SMART TRAVELER
   ENROLLMENT PROGRAM (STEP) ON WWW.TRAVEL.STATE.GOV.
10. VESSELS SHOULD REGISTER FOR ACCESS TO MSCHOA WEBSITE AT
    HTTP://WWW.MSCHOA.ORG TO OBTAIN UP TO DATE INFORMATION
    OF THE CIRCUMSTANCES AND CONDITIONS IN THE REGION.
11. FOR FURTHER INFORMATION REGARDING THIS ADVISORY, CONTACT
    CAPTAIN ROBERT FORD, MARITIME ADMINISTRATION, OFFICE OF
    SECURITY, CODE: MAR-420, ROOM W25-308, 1200 NEW JERSEY
    AVE, S.E., WASHINGTON, DC 20590, PHONE: 202 366 0223,
    FAX: 202 366 3954, TELEX: 710 822 9426 (MARAD DOT WSH)
    OR E-MAIL: MARADSECURITY@DOT.GOV.

EASTERN MEDITERRANEAN SEA.
LIBYA.
1. COALITION FORCES ARE CONDUCTING MILITARY OPERATIONS
   IN SUPPORT OF AN ARMS EMBARGO, NO-FLY ZONE AND OTHER
   MISSIONS TO PROTECT THE CIVILIAN POPULATION IN
   LIBYAN ARAB JAMAJIRIYA, AS AUTHORIZED BY UNITED NATIONS
   SECURITY COUNCIL RESOLUTION (UNSCR) 1973 IN THE
   MEDITERRANEAN SEA AND GULF OF SIDRA
   IN AN AREA BOUND BY
   33-08-00N 011-32-00E, 33-19-00N 011-13-00E,
   34-12-38N,011-12-25E, 35-35-55N 011-31-35E,
   36-30-00N 011-10-00E, 36-30-00N 022-00-00E,
   33-45-00N 025-09-00E, 31-40-00N 025-09-00E.
   EXCLUDING THE LAND AND TERRITORIAL SEAS OF
   THE REPUBLIC OF MALTA.
2. NAVAL VESSELS ARE CONDUCTING OPERATIONS IN
   INTERNATIONAL WATERS AND LIBYAN ARAB JAMAJIRIYA
   TERRITORIAL SEAS AND MAY POSE A HAZARD TO NAVIGATION.
   ALL VESSELS ARE ADVISED TO EXERCISE EXTREME CAUTION
   WHEN SAILING IN THE VICINITY OF, OR INTO OR OUT OF,
   THIS DESIGNATED AREA.
3. IN ACCORDANCE WITH PARAGRAPH THIRTEEN OF UNSCR 1973,
   MEMBER STATE MILITARY FORCES ARE AUTHORIZED TO INSPECT
   VESSELS IN THE TERRITORY OF LIBYAN ARAB JAMAJIRIYA,
   INCLUDING SEAPORTS, AND ON THE HIGH SEAS BOUND TO OR
   FROM THE LIBYAN ARAB JAMAJIRIYA IF THERE IS INFORMATION
   THAT PROVIDES REASONABLE GROUNDS TO BELIEVE THAT THE
   CARGO CONTAINS ARMS AND OTHER MATERIEL, THE SUPPLY,
   SALE, TRANSFER OR EXPORT OF WHICH IS PROHIBITED BY
   PARAGRAPHS NINE AND TEN OF UNSCR 1970 AS MODIFIED
   BY UNSCR 1973.
4. VESSELS FOUND TO BE CARRYING PROHIBITED ITEMS BOUND
   TO OR FROM LIBYAN ARAB JAMAJIRIYA ARE SUBJECT TO
   DETENTION AND THEIR CARGO SUBJECT TO SEIZURE.
5. FURTHER, COALITION FORCES ARE CONDUCTING MILITARY
   OPERATIONS IN THIS AREA IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE
   AUTHORIZATIONS STATED IN PARAGRAPHS FOUR AND EIGHT OF
   UNSCR 1973. VESSELS TRANSITING THESE WATERS DO SO AT
   INCREASED RISK. THE TIMELY AND ACCURATE IDENTIFICATION
   OF ALL VESSELS AND AIRCRAFT IN THE OPERATION AREA IS
   CRITICAL TO AVOID THE INADVERTENT USE OF FORCE. ALL
   VESSELS ARE ADVISED THAT COALITION FORCES ARE PREPARED
   TO EXERCISE SELF-DEFENSE MEASURES AS MAY BE NECESSARY
   TO ENSURE THEIR SAFETY IN THE EVENT THEY ARE APPROACHED
   BY VESSELS OR AIRCRAFT WHOSE INTENTIONS ARE AMBIGUOUS.
   COALITION FORCES ARE PREPARED TO RESPOND DECISIVELY TO
   ANY HOSTILE ACTS OR INDICATIONS OF HOSTILE INTENT. ALL
   MARITIME VESSELS OR ACTIVITIES THAT ARE DETERMINED TO
   BE THREATS TO COALITION FORCES WILL BE SUBJECT TO
   DEFENSIVE MEASURES, INCLUDING BOARDING, SEIZURE,
   DISABLING, OR DESTRUCTION, WITHOUT REGARD TO REGISTRY
   OR LOCATION.
6. ALL VESSELS SHOULD MAINTAIN A SAFE DISTANCE FROM NAVAL
   VESSELS CONDUCTING MILITARY OPERATIONS IN SUPPORT OF
   THE ARMS EMBARGO, THE NO-FLY ZONE, AND OTHER MISSIONS
   TO PROTECT THE CIVILIAN POPULATION IN LIBYAN ARAB
   JAMAJIRIYA SO THAT INTENTIONS ARE CLEAR AND UNDERSTOOD
   BY THE NAVAL FORCES. ALL SURFACE VESSELS, ALL SUBSURFACE
   VESSELS, AND ALL AIRCRAFT APPROACHING COALITION FORCES
   ARE REQUESTED TO MAINTAIN RADIO CONTACT ON BRIDGE TO
   BRIDGE CHANNEL VHF 16, INTERNATIONAL AIR DISTRESS
   (121.5 MHZ VHF) OR MILITARY AIR DISTRESS (243.0 MHZ UHF).

1. IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE UNITED NATIONS SECURITY COUNCIL
   RESOLUTION 1970 (26/02/2011) AND 1973 (17/03/2011),
   NATO MARITIME FORCES ARE CONDUCTING ARMS EMBARGO
   OPERATIONS IN THE VICINITY OF LIBYA TERRITORIAL
   WATERS.
2. THOSE OPERATIONS ARE CONDUCTED IN THE MEDITERRANEAN
   SEA AND SYSTEMATICALLY ENFORCED IN THE FOLLOWING
   MARITIME SURVEILLANCE AREA:
   A. NORTHERN BORDER: THE 35N PARALLEL.
   B. WESTERN BORDER: TUNISIAN TERRITORIAL WATERS
      (NOT INCLUDED).
   C. EASTERN BORDER: EGYPTIAN TERRITORIAL WATERS
      (NOT INCLUDED).
   D. SOUTHERN BORDER: LIBYAN TERRITORIAL WATERS
      (NOT INCLUDED).
3. ALL MERCHANT VESSELS TRANSITING IN THIS AREA OR
   INBOUND TO LIBYA TERRITORIAL WATERS MAY BE SUBJECT TO
   HAILING, QUERIES OR BOARDING FROM NATO NAVAL AND AIR
   UNITS. ALL MERCHANT VESSELS TRANSITING THROUGH THIS
   MARITIME SURVEILLANCE AREA ARE REQUESTED, 24 HR PRIOR
   THEIR ENTERING IN THIS AREA, TO CONTACT THE NATO
   SHIPPING CENTRE (UNITED KINGDOM, CONTACT DETAILS BELOW):
   EITHER VIA PHONE, FAX OR E-MAIL TO PROVIDE A FORMAT
   ALFA REPORT. THE REPORT MUST INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING
   INFORMATION:
   A. IMO NUMBER.
   B. DTG.
   C. POSITION, SPEED AND COURSE.
   D. LAST PORT OF CALL.
   E. NEXT PORT OF CALL.
   F. PORT OF REGISTRY.
   ALTERNATIVELY, A FORMAT ALFA FORM (REF ATP 2(B) IS
   AVAILABLE AT: (HTTP:WWW.SHIPPING.NATO.INT).
   ALSO, ALL MERCHANT VESSELS INBOUND TO LIBYA
   TERRITORIAL WATERS ARE REQUESTED, WHEN CROSSING THE
   BOUNDARIES OF THE MARITIME SURVEILLANCE AREA, TO
   CONTACT THE HEAD QUARTERS MARITIME COMMAND, NAPLES,
   MARITIME OPERATIONS CENTRE (ITALY, CONTACT DETAILS
   BELOW):
   EITHER VIA PHONE, FAX OR E-MAIL TO PROVIDE THE
   FOLLOWING INFORMATION:
   A. IMO NUMBER.
   B. LAST PORT OF CALL.
   C. NEXT PORT OF CALL.
   D. PORT OF REGISTRY.
4. THIS REQUEST FOR REPORTS FULLY SUPPORTS THE EMBARGO
   OPERATIONS UNDER THE UNITED NATIONS SECURITY COUNCIL
   RESOLUTION AND IS, THEREFORE, MANDATORY.
   THIS MEASURE IS COMPLEMENTARY OF THE ACTION OF NATO
   NAVAL FORCES IN THE VICINITY OF LIBYA TERRITORIAL
   WATERS.
   NATO PRIORITY IS TO REDUCE INTERFERENCES AND DELAYS
   CAUSED TO MERCHANT SHIPPING TRAFFIC TO ITS MINIMUM.
   IT IS THEREFORE HIGHLY RECOMMENDED FOR MERCHANT
   VESSELS TRANSITING THROUGH THIS AREA TO COOPERATE
   PROMPTLY WITH NATO FORCES.
   CONSEQUENTLY, FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THAT GUIDANCE
   WILL RESULT IN FURTHER INVESTIGATIONS CONDUCTED BY
   NATO NAVAL UNITS. THIS INVESTIGATION WILL INCLUDE
   DETAILED QUERIES, BOARDINGS OR EVEN DIVERSION TO
   NEARBY PORTS FOR INSPECTION AND WILL CAUSE MAJOR
   TRANSIT DELAYS TO MERCHANT SHIPPING.
5. POINTS OF CONTACT:
   THIS OPERATION IS CONDUCTED BY THE NATO HEADQUARTERS
   MARITIME COMMAND LOCATED IN NAPLES, ITALY.
   IT'S MARITIME OPERATIONS CENTER IS THE MAIN POINT OF
   CONTACT FOR ANY ISSUE OR INFORMATION REGARDING THE

EASTERN MEDITERRANEAN SEA.
LIBYA.
1. COMMAND AND CONTROL OF NATO MARITIME EMBARGO
   OPERATION NATO HAS ASSUMED THE ROLE OF
   COORDINATOR FOR THE MARITIME EMBARGO OPERATIONS
   IN VICINITY OF LIBYAN ARAB JAMAHIRIYA (LIBYA)
   IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE UNITED NATION SECURITY
   RESOLUTION (UNSCR) 1970 AND 1973. THERE
   CONTINUES TO BE A LARGE NUMBER OF NAVAL VESSELS
   OPERATING IN THE AREA.
2. MARITIME SURVEILLANCE AREA (MSA) AND MARITIME
   INTERDICTION OPERATIONS (MIO) ARE CONDUCTED IN
   THE MEDITERRANEAN SEA AND SYSTEMATICALLY
   ENFORCED IN THE FOLLOWING MARITIME
   SURVEILLANCE AREA (MSA):
   A. NORTHERN LIMIT: 34-00N
   B. WESTERN LIMIT:  34-00N 012-00E
   C. SOUTHERN LIMIT: LIBYA TTW LIMIT (NOT INCLUDED)
   D. EASTERN LIMITS: 34-00N 022-00E AND 33-00N 025-00E
3. REPORTING SYSTEM IN ORDER TO REDUCE TO A MINIMUM THE
   DISRUPTIONS TO MERCHANT TRAFFIC, THIS EMBARGO IS RUN
   USING A SYSTEM BASED ON THE DISTANCE OF A M/V FROM
   THE MARITIME SURVEILLANCE AREA AND THE VESSEL'S NEXT
   PORT OF CALL (NPOC). THE REPORTING SYSTEM IS UPDATED
   AS FOLLOWS: ALL M/V PLANNING TO TRANSIT THROUGH THE
   MSA WILL SEND A FORMAT A (REF ATP 2(B) IS AVAILABLE
   AT: HTTP:WWW.SHIPPING.NATO.INT) 
   MESSAGE TO THE NATO SHIPPING CENTRE (UK) 12 HOURS
   PRIOR TO ENTERING THE MSA. UPON ENTERING THE MSA,
   VESSEL WITH A NPOC IN LIBYA MUST SEND AN ADDITIONAL
   FORMAT MESSAGE TO THE NATO HQ MC NAPLES (ITALY).
   ALL M/V LEAVING A LIBYAN HARBOUR WILL SEND A FORMAT
   MESSAGE TO THE NATO HQ MC NAPLES SIX HOURS PRIOR TO
   SAILING FROM THE HARBOUR. TRANSMISSION CHANNELS
   REMAIN THE SAME: E-MAILS, PHONE CALL ETC. CF PARA 5.
4. GENERAL GUIDANCE FOR MARINERS: NATO IS ENFORCING THIS
   ARMS EMBARGO AND WILL BE PROACTIVE IN MEASURES TO
   DETERMINE A VESSEL'S CARGO AND NPOC. NATO NAVAL UNITS
   ARE FULLY COMPLYING WITH INTERNATIONAL LAWS AND
   CONVENTIONS. MERCHANT SHIPS IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE
   ARMS EMBARGO HAVE NO REASON TO FEAR NATO NAVAL UNITS.
   WILLINGNESS TO COOPERATE AND QUICKLY RESPOND TO NATO
   UNIT HAILING IS HIGHLY ENCOURAGED IN ORDER TO MINIMIZE
   DELAYS TO TRAFFIC FLOW. A NAVAL COOPERATION AND
   GUIDANCE FOR SHIPPING (NCAGS) CELL CAN BE CONTACTED
   24/7 ( 24 HOURS / 7 DAYS PER WEEK) IN THE HEAD
   QUARTERS MARITIME COMMAND (HQ MC) NAPLES-MARITIME
   OPERATIONS CENTRE (MOC). THIS CELL'S MAIN TASK IS TO
   ENHANCE COORDINATION BETWEEN NATO UNITS ENFORCING THE
   EMBARGO AND MARINERS TRANSITING THROUGH THE MARITIME
   SURVEILLANCE AREA. HQ MC NAPLES STRONGLY RECOMMENDS
   TO MERCHANT COMPANIES PLANNING THEIR SHIPS TO CALL
   IN LIBYAN HARBOURS TO LIAISE FIRST WITH NCAGSCELL.
5. POINTS OF CONTACT:
   THIS OPERATION IS CONDUCTED BY THE NATO HEADQUARTERS
   MARITIME COMMAND LOCATED IN NAPLES, ITALY.
   IT'S MARITIME OPERATIONS CENTER IS THE MAIN POINT OF
   CONTACT FOR ANY ISSUE OR INFORMATION REGARDING THE
   ARMS EMBARGO:
   HQ MC NAPLES MOC PHONE: 800 1101 2010.
   HQ MC NAPLES MOC UNCLASSIFIED INTERNET ADDRESS:
   MOCN3DOGCELL@MANP.NATO.INT
   FOR OTHER ISSUES, THE NATO SHIPPING CENTRE
   (NORTHWOOD, UNITED KINGDOM), REMAINS THE MAIN POC,
   PHONE: 44 192 395 6574, FAX: 44 192 395 6575,
   E-MAIL: INFO@SHIPPING.NATO.INT.
6. CANCEL HYDROLANT 492/11.

   BY THE ISRAELI NAVY IN AREA BOUND BY
   31-46.80N 034-10.01E, 31-35.71N 034-29.46E,
   31-19.39N 034-13.11E, 31-33.73N 033-56.68E.

   AREA ARE LIKELY TO FACE ENFORCEMENT ACTION BY THE
   ISRAELI NAVY. THE DEPARTMENT OF STATE HAS ALSO ADVISED
   AGAINST TRAVEL BY U.S. CITIZENS TO GAZA BY ANY MEANS,
   INCLUDING BY SEA. PREVIOUS ATTEMPTS TO ENTER GAZA BY
   SEA HAVE RESULTED IN VIOLENT INCIDENTS AND THE DETENTION
   AND DEPORTATION OF THOSE INVOLVED.
3. U.S. MARINERS ARE ALSO REMINDED THAT PROCEDURES EXIST
   FOR THE DELIVERY OF HUMANITARIAN CARGO TO GAZA VIA THE
   ISRAELI PORT OF ASHDOD OR THE EGYPTIAN PORT OF EL-ARISH,
   WHERE CARGO CAN BE SCREENED.

PANAMA CANAL.
1. MULTI-NATION NAVAL CONTROL OF SHIPPING (NCS) AUTHORITIES
   WILL BE CONDUCTING AN EXERCISE FROM 19-24 AUGUST 2011.
   THE EXERCISE WILL BE ONGOING IN THE BROAD VICINITY OF
   APPROACHES TO THE PANAMA CANAL.
2. AN NCAGS REGION WILL BE DECLARED EFFECTIVE AS OF 19 AUGUST
   IN THE INTERNATIONAL AND COASTAL WATERS APPROACHING THE
   PANAMA CANAL, BOTH ATLANTIC AND PACIFIC.  A MULTI-NATIONAL
   COALITION NAVAL FORCE AND NOTIONAL RISK AREAS WILL BE
   PRESENT IN THESE WATERS. THE REGION WILL BE DISESTABLISHED
   AS OF 24 AUGUST 2011.
3. MERCHANT AND COMMERCIAL SHIPS TRANSITING THESE AREAS MAY
   BE REQUESTED TO REPORT VESSEL INFORMATION UTILIZING FORMAT
   ALPHA REPORTING FORMAT AS OUTLINED IN ALLIED TECHNICAL
   PUBLICATION ATP2(B) VOL 2- NAVAL CO-OPERATION AND GUIDANCE
   FOR SHIPPING MANUAL (NCAGS)- GUIDE TO OWNERS, OPERATORS,
   MASTERS AND OFFICERS. BASIC INFORMATION INCLUDES:
   A. SHIP NAME
   B. CALLSIGN/IMO#
   C. E-MAIL ADDRESS OF VESSEL
   D. TYPE OF SHIP (E.G. TANKER, CONTAINER, BULK, FISHING.)
   E. TYPE OF CARGO
   F. POSITION, COURSE AND SPEED AT TIME OF REPORTING
   G. PREVIOUS PORT OF CALL
   H. NEXT PORT OF CALL
   SHIP INFORMATION WILL BE HANDLED AS BUSINESS AND SECURITY
   SENSITIVE INFORMATION. REPORTS DURING THE EXERCISE SHOULD
   BE MADE TO THE SHIPPING COORDINATOR AT THE FOLLOWING
   E-MAIL ADDRESS: SCT@NAVYTELEX.US
4. SAFETY IS PARAMOUNT. THE EXERCISE WILL NOT EMPLOY ACTUAL
   CHANGES TO MARSEC LEVEL OR SHIPBOARD EMERGENCY ALERT
   SIGNALS. NAVAL GUIDANCE TO MERCHANT SHIPPING IS ADVISORY
   ONLY. MERCHANT PARTICIPATION IS STRICTLY VOLUNTARY.
5. MERCHANCT VESSELS INTERESTED IN VOLUNTARY EXERCISE
   PARTICIPATION DURING THE EXERCISE PERIOD SHOULD CONTACT
   JECG@NAVYTELEX.US. THE HIGHEST CONSIDERATION FOR VOYAGE
   SCHEDULES AND EFFICIENCIES IS RECOGNIZED. AS PRACTICAL,
   NCAGS WILL ASSIST DOCUMENTATION OF SHIP PARTICIPATION
   FOR SECURITY DRILL PURPOSES.
6. VESSELS WITHIN THE NCAGS SHIPPING AREA DURING THE EXERCISE
   DATES WITH QUESTIONS OR CONCERNS CAN CONTACT THE PANAMA
   LOCATED NCAGS SHIPPING CONTROL TEAM (SCT) VIA
   E-MAIL TO: SCT@NAVYTELEX.US.
7. FOR FURTHER INFORMATION REGARDING THIS ADVISORY, CONTACT
   CAPTAIN ROBERT FORD, MARITIME ADMINISTRATION, OFFICE OF
   SECURITY, CODE: MAR-420, ROOM W25-308, 1200 NEW JERSEY AVE, S.E.,
   WASHINGTION, DC 20590, PHONE: 202 366 0223, FAX: 202 366 3954,
   TELEX: 7108229426 (MARAD DOT WSH) OR E-MAIL: MARADSECURITY@DOT.GOV.
8. CANCEL THIS MSG 25 AUG.
